claim: Running AI agents in containers (Podman/Docker) provides reproducibility, secret isolation, portability, and natural sandbox isolation compared to native installation.
actionable: Containerize OpenClaw or other agents using Podman/Docker; use Podman secrets to isolate API keys rather than storing them as plain environment variables.
Use Podman/Docker for agent deployment to gain reproducibility, secret isolation, and portability across environments.
source_title: Lobster Trap: OpenClaw in Containers from Local to K8s and Back

claim: OpenClaw agent containers should use a secrets layer with double indirection: Podman secrets for API keys on the host, plus OpenClaw secret refs inside the container for additional separation.
actionable: Set up Podman secrets at the host level, then reference them in the container using OpenClaw's secret ref feature to create layered secret management.
source_title: Lobster Trap: OpenClaw in Containers from Local to K8s and Back

claim: A curated baseline OpenClaw with company-approved MCP servers, authentication, and team-specific skills can be containerized and distributed to new hires for reproducible, standardized onboarding.
actionable: Create a container image with your organization's standard OpenClaw setup (baseline MCP servers, approved auth, team skills) and use it as the onboarding standard for new team members.
source_title: Lobster Trap: OpenClaw in Containers from Local to K8s and Back

claim: Spinning up an OpenClaw container via Podman command takes approximately 1-2 seconds, enabling rapid agent instantiation for testing and deployment.
actionable: Leverage fast container startup for rapid iteration on agent configurations and testing; avoid assumptions about slow deployment cycles.
source_title: Lobster Trap: OpenClaw in Containers from Local to K8s and Back

claim: The same container image and configuration approach that works locally with Podman can be scaled to Kubernetes clusters (Kind, OpenShift) with minimal changes by using Kubernetes secrets and PVCs.
actionable: Design containerized agent setups to be Kubernetes-compatible from the start; use volume mounts and secret refs that translate directly from Podman to K8s.
source_title: Lobster Trap: OpenClaw in Containers from Local to K8s and Back

claim: An Nvidia team runs model evals with OpenClaw in Kubernetes across 10 engineers, each with their own instance, delivering work that previously required 6 engineers—demonstrating AI agent productivity multiplier.
actionable: Consider distributed agent architectures for team-scale evaluation and automation work; agents running in containers can achieve 6x human productivity in the right domain.
source_title: Lobster Trap: OpenClaw in Containers from Local to K8s and Back
